description: Fix v2 review issues: (1) getTokensForUser returns objects with docPath for stale token cleanup, (2) cleanupFCMToken only removes current device token, (3) Use collectionGroup queries to eliminate N+1 reads.
steps: Step 1 - Fix getTokensForUser in functions/index.js: Return {token, docPath} objects instead of bare strings so sendToTokens can clean up stale tokens.
Step 2 - Update callers of getTokensForUser in functions/index.js: onNotifikasiCreated and onMeetingInviteCreated should use the returned objects directly instead of wrapping with docPath: null.
Step 3 - Fix cleanupFCMToken in core.js: Get the current FCM token, compute its hash, and delete only that device document instead of all devices.

Step 4 - Use collectionGroup queries in functions/index.js: Replace sequential reads in getAllTokens with db.collectionGroup('devices').get() and replace the role-based N+1 query with db.collectionGroup('devices').where('role', '==', targetRole).get().
acceptance_criteria: getTokensForUser returns array of {token, docPath} objects
onNotifikasiCreated and onMeetingInviteCreated pass getTokensForUser results directly to sendToTokens
cleanupFCMToken deletes only the current device's token document (hrd_fcm_tokens/{userId}/devices/{hash})
getAllTokens uses db.collectionGroup('devices') instead of sequential reads
All JS files pass syntax check
